headline: Venezuela, Chevron strike deals to expand oil operations in key region
summary:
US oil giant Chevron and the government of Venezuela signed two deals Monday that will expand oil production in the country, as the country seeks to boost private investment in the sector.In return, the United States eased sanctions on Venezuela's oil industry, expanding the ability of US companies to operate in the country.
pgf-atm-tmc/pnb/ksb

headline: Chevron Deepens Venezuela Footprint With Strategic Asset Swap
summary: Chevron has agreed to an asset swap with Venezuela’s state oil company PDVSA to consolidate its position in key heavy oil projects while divesting selected gas and non-core assets.

content.title: Chevron Deepens Venezuela Footprint With Strategic Asset Swap
content.summary: Chevron has agreed to an asset swap with Venezuela’s state oil company PDVSA to consolidate its position in key heavy oil projects while divesting selected gas and non-core assets.

headline: Chevron Venezuela Asset Swap Reshapes Heavy Oil Exposure And Valuation Story
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) has agreed to increase its ownership in the Petroindependencia joint venture in Venezuela to 49%. The company has also secured development rights in the Orinoco Oil Belt, one of the world's largest heavy oil reserves. The deal is taking place as US sanctions on Venezuela are eased, allowing Chevron more operational flexibility in the country. The agreement involves state oil company PDVSA and focuses on heavy crude production and long term development potential. For...

headline: Chevron increases Venezuela heavy oil stake through asset swap with PDVSA
summary: Chevron will transfer its 60% and full ownership interests in Plataforma Deltana Block 21 and Block 32 to Venezuelan entities.

content.title: Chevron increases Venezuela heavy oil stake through asset swap with PDVSA
content.summary: Chevron will transfer its 60% and full ownership interests in Plataforma Deltana Block 21 and Block 32 to Venezuelan entities.

thumbnail.caption: Chevron will divest its 25.2% non-operated stake in the Petroindependiente JV in western Venezuela. Credit: tishomir/Shutterstock.com.

headline: Energy Transition Today - Solar Surge Asia Leads Renewable Energy Growth
summary: The "Strategic Intelligence: Renewable Energy (2026)" report projects a significant expansion in global renewable energy capacity, anticipated to more than double from 4,107GW in 2025 to 8,430GW by 2031. The rapid growth is largely driven by solar photovoltaic (PV) becoming the largest source of renewable electricity globally, with notable dominance in the Asia-Pacific region, particularly China. This development underscores a major shift in the global power mix, highlighting considerable...

headline: Chevron vs. ConocoPhillips: Only One of These Energy Dividends Is Safe to Hold Forever
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) and ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) closed 2025 with results that expose a fundamental divide in how two oil giants handle commodity downturns. One relies on a diversified, integrated model. The other bets on pure-play discipline and shale inventory. The dividend question cuts to the heart of that difference.
